response.justification: Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with solid infrastructure and distributed systems experience (Dunnhumby's real-time microservices platform), proven leadership ability, and strong software engineering practices. However, he falls critically short on the role's primary technical requirement: 8 years of C++ programming. His C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments in 2015 plus thesis work), and his career has been dominated by web/product engineering (Node.js, TypeScript, React). While his DevOps and Kubernetes knowledge are relevant, his expertise lies in building products that consume cloud services rather than building cloud compute platforms themselves.
strengths: Extensive infrastructure and DevOps experience (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI, deployment servers) demonstrated across multiple roles
Proven large-scale distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second with microservices and real-time data processing
Strong leadership and mentoring track record through staff roles, founder status, workshops, and courses; demonstrates ability to establish best practices and own complex projects
concerns: Critical gap in C++ expertise: resume shows only ~6 months of C++ work (2014-2015) versus the 8-year minimum requirement; primary language stack is JavaScript/TypeScript
Limited cloud platform architecture focus; experience is primarily in product engineering with cloud services (AWS CDK) rather than building cloud compute platforms
No demonstrated experience in technical leadership specifically mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers; leadership roles are primarily in web/product and consulting contexts
